summary: The test suite is well-organized across six focused test classes that map cleanly to each major feature: glob file finding, single-file search, preview mode, backup creation, replacement with report, and error handling. All core requirements from the task description are exercised, earning high coverage marks. Design is excellent: each class uses IDisposable temp-directory fixtures for isolation, a concise CreateFile helper eliminates boilerplate, tests follow the Arrange-Act-Assert pattern with clear names, and test independence is maintained throughout. Rigor is the weakest dimension — the happy paths and a reasonable set of boundary conditions are covered (context at file start/end, multiple matches per line, case sensitivity, regex groups, empty directory, invalid regex), but several meaningful edge cases are absent: (1) no test that backups are created only for files that actually match (not all searched files), (2) no test verifying backup-then-replace ordering, (3) no test for empty files, (4) no test for the non-preview + no-replacement (search-only without PreviewOnly=true) path through Run, (5) no test that preview mode with CreateBackups=true does NOT create backups, and (6) the 'preview separator ---' formatting is never asserted. These gaps could allow subtle regressions in backup/preview interaction to go undetected.
